content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 11, 1990

Jeanne C. Pavao
Corporate Counsel
Diablo Construction Co., Inc.
4021 Port Chicago Highway
P.O. Box 4113
Concord, CA  94524-4113

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-90-687

Dear Ms. Pavao:

     You have requested advice on behalf of Diablo Construction
Co. concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act.¹ In our telephone conversation of November 15, 1990, you
withdrew your request for written advice for two of the three
questions listed in your letter.

QUESTION

     Must contributions to a county supervisorial candidate made
by subcontractors of Diablo Construction Co., and at the request
of Diablo Construction Co., be reported on Diablo Construction
Company's major donor statement?

CONCLUSION

     Unless the same person or group of persons, in fact, directs
and controls the contributions of both Diablo Construction Co. and
a contributing subcontractor, or a subcontractor acts as an
intermediary or agent for Diablo Construction Co., Diablo
Construction Company is not required to report the contributions
of any subcontractor.

FACTS

     In a letter from the campaign manager of a candidate for
county supervisor, Diablo Construction Co., which is a general
contractor, was requested to contact its subcontractors to solicit
contributions for the candidate. Contributions from the

     ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Jeanne C. Pavao
Page Two

subcontractors would be made directly from the subcontractor to
the candidate.

ANALYSIS

A major donor committee is required to report contributions
and expenditures it makes. (Section 84211.) Contributions made
by a subcontractor of Diablo Construction Co. must be reported on
Diablo Construction's major donor statement if the same person or
group of persons, in fact, directs and controls the contributions
of both the subcontractor and Diablo Construction Co. (Regulation
18428, copy enclosed.) Alternatively, if the subcontractor makes
the contribution on behalf of Diablo Construction Co. as an agent,
the contribution would have to be reported by Diablo Construction
Co. In addition, if the subcontractor acts as an intermediary for
Diablo Construction Company's contributions, i.e., the
subcontractor makes contributions using Diablo Construction Co.
funds or there is an agreement that the subcontractor's
contribution will be reimbursed by Diablo Construction Co., such
contributions must be reported by Diablo Construction Co.

Additionally, if Diablo Construction Co. incurs any costs in
soliciting its subcontractors at the behest of the candidate,
these costs must be reported by Diablo Construction Co. as in-kind
contributions to the candidate. (Regulation 18215.)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
